name: Java
description: Java is a statically typed general-purpose language designed for portability through the Java Virtual Machine.
history: Java was created at Sun Microsystems by James Gosling and released in 1995. It became widely used in enterprise software, Android development, and large-scale backend systems.

name: JavaScript
description: JavaScript is a dynamically typed language primarily used for interactive web applications, servers, and tooling.
history: JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich in 1995 for Netscape Navigator. It evolved from a browser scripting language into one of the most widely used languages across frontend and backend development.

name: C
description: C is a statically typed systems programming language known for low-level memory access and high performance.
history: C was developed by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs in the early 1970s. It became foundational for operating systems, compilers, embedded software, and many later programming languages.

name: C++
description: C++ is a statically typed language that extends C with abstractions for object-oriented, generic, and high-performance programming.
history: C++ was created by Bjarne Stroustrup as an extension of C. Since its release in the 1980s it has been heavily used in game engines, desktop applications, finance, and performance-critical systems.

name: C#
description: C# is a statically typed modern language in the .NET ecosystem used for web, desktop, cloud, and game development.
history: C# was developed by Microsoft under the leadership of Anders Hejlsberg and introduced in 2000. It has grown from a Windows-focused language into a cross-platform language used throughout the .NET platform.

name: Python
description: Python is a dynamically typed general-purpose language valued for readability, productivity, and a large ecosystem.
history: Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. It became a dominant language in scripting, web development, automation, data science, and machine learning.

name: TypeScript
description: TypeScript is a statically typed superset of JavaScript that adds types and tooling support for large applications.
history: TypeScript was developed by Microsoft and released in 2012. It became popular as a way to scale JavaScript projects while still compiling to standard JavaScript for broad runtime support.

name: Go Lang
description: Go is a statically typed language designed for simplicity, concurrency, and reliable backend and infrastructure software.
history: Go was created at Google by Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, and Ken Thompson. Since its public release in 2009 it has become common in cloud services, developer tools, and distributed systems.

name: Kotlin
description: Kotlin is a statically typed language that targets the JVM and other platforms with concise syntax and strong interoperability.
history: Kotlin was created by JetBrains and publicly introduced in 2011. It gained broad adoption after becoming an officially supported language for Android development.

name: Swift
description: Swift is a statically typed language developed by Apple for building applications across its platforms with safety and speed.
history: Swift was introduced by Apple in 2014 as a successor to Objective-C for iOS and macOS development. It expanded into server-side and cross-platform use while remaining strongest in the Apple ecosystem.

name: Rust
description: Rust is a statically typed systems language focused on memory safety, concurrency, and performance without a garbage collector.
history: Rust began as a Mozilla research project led by Graydon Hoare and was first announced in 2010. It has since gained strong adoption for safe systems programming, tooling, and infrastructure.
